messages.content:
Create a retrieval-oriented summary for this parent section of a document.
Use the child summaries below. Mention the topics, entities, dates, tables,
figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, and evidence types covered
when they are visible. Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

Parent:
{
  "node_id": "0105",
  "title": "corp.funtap.vn早期留存与规模化准备关键建议",
  "page_index": 54,
  "page_range": "54"
}

Child summaries:
[
  {
    "node_id": "0106",
    "title": "通过混合式引导提升 D1留存率 >40%",
    "page_range": "54",
    "summary": "This section outlines strategies to mitigate early player churn and improve Day 1 (D1) retention in city-building games. \n**Topics & Entities:** Game onboarding, D1 retention, FTUE (First Time User Experience), city-building games, 4X games.\n**Claims & Metrics:** Traditional city-building games suffer from high early churn; implementing hybrid onboarding can increase D1 retention by >40%.\n**Procedures & Timeframes:** FTUE should be structured within the first 15 minutes of gameplay, integrating core operations, puzzles, or cinematic moments to drive strong engagement, following proven 4X game benchmarks.\n**Definitions:** FTUE = First Time User Experience.\n**Answerable Questions:** How can early player loss be reduced in city-building games? What is the target metric for D1 retention improvement? What should FTUE include and how long should it last? Which industry design standards should onboarding follow?\n**Tables, Figures & Evidence Types:** None present in this excerpt."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0107",
    "title": "通过强制社交化稳固 D7留存",
    "page_range": "54",
    "summary": "This section addresses strategies for stabilizing Day 7 (D7) retention in 4X games through mandatory social mechanics. It can answer questions regarding:\n- **Topics & Claims:** How forced socialization impacts player retention; the assertion that guild participation is the primary driver of D7 retention in 4X games.\n- **Procedures:** Making alliance joining a mandatory main quest within the first 24 hours of gameplay; requiring team-based play for core events.\n- **Entities & Timeframes:** 4X games, alliances/guilds, core events, main quests, the initial 24-hour onboarding window, and Day 7 (D7).\n- **Metrics:** D7 retention rate.\n- **Evidence Types:** Strategic game design recommendation/industry assertion (no empirical data, tables, or figures are provided).\n- **Definitions:** None explicitly provided.\nThe content functions as a design guideline for onboarding and social feature implementation to boost mid-term retention."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0108",
    "title": "通过早期时间压缩培养习惯",
    "page_range": "54",
    "summary": "本章节可回答以下检索问题：\n- **主题**：早期游戏阶段如何通过时间压缩策略培养玩家习惯。\n- **实体**：加速道具。\n- **时间节点**：第一周（进度加速期）、第二周（经济收紧期）、每日（习惯周期）。\n- **操作流程**：第一周大量使用加速道具推进进度以避免长时间等待；在第二周经济机制收紧前，优先建立每日固定时长的游戏习惯。\n- **核心主张**：早期时间压缩能有效促进习惯养成；需在第二周经济环境变化前完成习惯铺垫。\n- **关键指标**：每日游戏时长约60分钟。\n- **图表与证据类型**：本节未提供表格、图表或外部证据。"
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0109",
    "title": "付费流量验证规模化能力",
    "page_range": "54-55",
    "summary": "**Topics:** Paid user acquisition (UA) validation for product scalability, mobile game marketing strategy, creative deployment trends, sub-gameplay UA tactics.\n**Entities:** Funtap Games, 广大大 (Advertiser Analysis Module), strategy mobile games, parkour sub-gameplay.\n**Dates:** January 2025 to December 2025 (data coverage period for market trends).\n**Procedures & Claims:** Recommends validating scalability using paid UA metrics instead of organic traffic. Claims that a Day 1 (D1) retention rate below 35% indicates the product is not ready for global scaling; this is a minimum threshold, and true scalable growth requires significantly higher retention. Advises initiating paid UA testing early in the product lifecycle.\n**Metrics & Thresholds:** D1 retention benchmark: <35% (not scalable), ≥35% (minimum viable), significantly higher for optimal scaling. Market metrics include monthly advertiser counts (2.5K–4.0K range), percentage of advertisers launching new creatives (~51%–86%), monthly average active creatives (0–400 range), and percentage of new creatives (~51%–59%).\n**Figures & Tables:** Two monthly trend charts (Jan–Dec 2025): (1) Advertiser count and proportion of advertisers deploying new creatives; (2) Monthly average active creatives and proportion of new creatives. Data sourced from 广大大 (Advertiser Analysis Module), tracking newly launched or high-heat ad creatives.\n**Evidence Types:** Industry benchmark/threshold (35% D1 retention), expert/company recommendation (Funtap Games), quantitative market data (2025 advertiser & creative volume trends), qualitative market observation (strategy games shifting to lightweight mechanics + asymmetric social features; parkour sub-gameplay dominating UA campaigns)."
  }
]

message.content:
This section provides strategic recommendations for optimizing early player retention and validating product scalability in city-building and 4X mobile games. 
**Topics & Entities:** Game onboarding, D1/D7 retention, FTUE, forced socialization, time compression, paid user acquisition (UA), Funtap Games, 广大大 (Advertiser Analysis Module), strategy games, parkour sub-gameplay, alliances/guilds, speed-up items.
**Definitions:** FTUE = First Time User Experience.
**Procedures & Timeframes:** Structure hybrid FTUE within the first 15 minutes of gameplay (integrating core operations, puzzles, or cinematics); mandate alliance/guild joining via main quests within the first 24 hours and require team-based participation for core events; heavily deploy speed-up items during Week 1 to eliminate wait times and establish a ~60-minute daily play habit before Week 2 economic tightening; initiate paid UA testing early in the product lifecycle.
**Claims:** Traditional city-building games experience high early churn; hybrid onboarding and mandatory social mechanics are primary drivers for improving D1 and stabilizing D7 retention, respectively; early time compression effectively conditions long-term player habits; paid UA metrics (not organic traffic) must validate scalability, with a D1 retention rate below 35% indicating the product is unready for global scaling.
**Metrics & Thresholds:** D1 retention target >40% (via hybrid onboarding) and ≥35% minimum viable threshold for scaling; D7 retention rate stabilization; ~60 minutes daily playtime; 2025 market metrics: 2.5K–4.0K monthly advertisers, ~51%–86% launching new creatives, 0–400 average active creatives, and ~51%–59% new creative proportion.
**Dates:** January 2025 to December 2025 (advertiser and creative trend coverage period).
**Tables, Figures & Evidence Types:** Includes two monthly trend charts (Jan–Dec 2025) tracking advertiser counts/new creative deployment and active/new creative volumes. Evidence comprises industry design benchmarks, Funtap Games strategic recommendations, quantitative market data from 广大大, and qualitative observations on lightweight mechanics and asymmetric social features in strategy games. No tables or figures are present in the onboarding, socialization, or habit-formation subsections.

**Claims:** Traditional city-building games experience high early churn; hybrid onboarding and mandatory social mechanics are primary drivers for improving D1 and stabilizing D7 retention, respectively; early time compression effectively conditions long-term player habits; paid UA metrics (not organic traffic) must validate scalability, with a D1 retention rate below 35% indicating the product is unready for global scaling.
**Metrics & Thresholds:** D1 retention target >40% (via hybrid onboarding) and ≥35% minimum viable threshold for scaling; D7 retention rate stabilization; ~60 minutes daily playtime; 2025 market metrics: 2.5K–4.0K monthly advertisers, ~51%–86% launching new creatives, 0–400 average active creatives, and ~51%–59% new creative proportion.
**Dates:** January 2025 to December 2025 (advertiser and creative trend coverage period).
**Tables, Figures & Evidence Types:** Includes two monthly trend charts (Jan–Dec 2025) tracking advertiser counts/new creative deployment and active/new creative volumes. Evidence comprises industry design benchmarks, Funtap Games strategic recommendations, quantitative market data from 广大大, and qualitative observations on lightweight mechanics and asymmetric social features in strategy games. No tables or figures are present in the onboarding, socialization, or habit-formation subsections.
